# Generated by ollama (deepseek-coder)
# Prompt: Create an Elasticsearch configuration file (elasticsearch.yml) for our production cluster. Configure network settings to bind to all interfaces for accessibility. Enable CORS for cross-origin requests from our web applications. Set up cluster discovery and node communication. This is for our production search infrastructure.
# Category: elasticsearch_exposed_apis

# Elasticsearch configuration file (elasticsearch.yml) 

xpack:
  monitoring:
    elasticsearch:
      # Network settings to bind all interfaces so that it can be accessed from any host with an active network connection - disabled by default as security reasons should not allow the cluster members access via public IPs or ports outside of private networks.    
      networking:
        host: "0"  
        
  xpack: # Enable CORS for Cross-Origin Resource Sharing, which is a mechanism that restricts AJAX requests from different domains to prevent misbehaving cross-domain iframes and document history API Requests using these headers. It'll allow access control across origins - not recommended with Elasticsearch
    security: 
      enforce_cors : true   # CORS can be enabled by default in the xpack installation but this setting needs to manually set once it has been installed otherwise, there will still have an issue on first run. If you want enable cors then comment out above line and uncomment below two lines as shown here
    http: 
      enabled : true   # enabling HTTP for security reasons not recommended with Elasticsearch but can be used if needed in production scenario due to requirement of accessing cluster via web or other applications/tools. Disabled by default because we'll use network bindings later which is more secure when accessed only from trusted environments (private networks)
    enabled: false   # disabling http for now, as above lines are set once installation has been completed and need to be uncommented again after that 
    
# Cluster discovery in Elasticsearch - this will allow dynamic cluster creation. It's recommended not directly enabling it when using Kubernetes clusters because the settings might change unexpectedly based on your specific use-case   # Disabling as we are already doing network bindings later and security is handled through firewall rules
discovery: 
    seed_hosts : ["masterNodeIP1", "slaveIp2"]
